compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Problem
The `rebar-brainstorm` skill (examples/agent-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md) tells the
author to run the plan-review gate on *each* ticket immediately after creating them, before
any dependency links exist and regardless of blocked state. This contradicts the actual gate
behavior documented in AGENTS.md / docs/plan-review-gate.md: `rebar review-plan` fast-fails
with no LLM (unsigned INDETERMINATE, exit 2) on a ticket that is not yet claimable — one still
`open` but blocked by an unclosed dependency — and reviews are dependency-scoped so they must
run in dependency order (prerequisites/children before dependents), never in parallel.

## Approach
Rewrite the "Before recording tickets, read the project's plan-review gate" section of
rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md so that, after creating tickets, the author FIRST establishes the
dependency graph between children (link via blocks/depends_on), then runs plan-review ONLY on
unblocked tickets (identified by `rebar ready`), in dependency order, reviewing later tickets
as their dependencies close and they become unblocked. Include a fallback: if `rebar ready`
returns an empty set, the graph likely has a cycle/authoring error — inspect and resolve before
proceeding. Rationale: authoring to the real gate protocol avoids a guaranteed no-LLM
fast-fail and matches AGENTS.md's dependency-order review rule; it is far cheaper than
discovering the exit-2 at review time.

Also assess rebar-implement/SKILL.md: it is an executor of an already-decomposed epic and
already claims/reviews only the unblocked, dependency-ordered set via `rebar ready`/
`next-batch`, treating the dependency graph as a precondition — so it needs no change. This
assessment is part of the ticket's scope (documented outcome: no edit, rationale recorded).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md's plan-review section states plan-review is run only on
  unblocked tickets, after the child dependency graph (blocks/depends_on links) is established,
  in dependency order — verified by reading the edited section (grep shows "unblocked" and
  "dependency order" in the section).
- [ ] The section references the no-LLM fast-fail (exit 2 / INDETERMINATE) on still-blocked
  tickets and names `rebar ready` for identifying the unblocked set — verified by reading the
  section.
- [ ] The section specifies a fallback for when `rebar ready` returns an empty set (diagnose
  the dependency graph for a cycle/authoring error and resolve before proceeding) — verified by
  reading the section.
- [ ] rebar-implement/SKILL.md is left byte-for-byte unchanged (verified by
  `git diff --stat origin/main -- examples/agent-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` showing no
  change), and the no-edit assessment rationale is recorded as a comment on this ticket.
- [ ] [operator-attested] Change lands on main through Gerrit — proven by recording the Gerrit
  change number and the LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1 vote outcomes as a comment on this ticket
  before close.

file_impact.reason: Rewrite the plan-review-gate section to run review only on unblocked tickets after the child dependency graph is established, in dependency order.
Assessed only; no edit — executor already reviews the unblocked, dependency-ordered set. Recorded for coverage.

compiled_state.title: rebar-brainstorm: run plan-review only on unblocked tickets after dependency graph is established
